perturbation.original_text: For a period of 18 months, COMPANY agrees not to disclose or disseminate the Confidential Information to any third party, except for those of its employees, lawyers, accountants, insurance agents and other professional representatives who have a need to know such Confidential Information for the purpose of COMPANY’S evaluation of the opportunity and who agree to abide by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of 18 months, COMPANY agrees not to disclose or disseminate the Sensitive Information to any third party, except for those of its employees, lawyers, accountants, insurance agents and other professional representatives who have a need to know such Confidential Information for the purpose of COMPANY’S evaluation of the opportunity and who agree to abide by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Substituting 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Information', while retaining 'Confidential Information' later in the same sentence, creates confusion. It is unclear whether 'Sensitive Information' falls under the definition of 'Confidential Information' or if it represents another undefined category of protected data. This is an in-text contradiction that creates potential disputes regarding what information is covered by the non-disclosure agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of 18 months, COMPANY agrees not to disclose or disseminate the Sensitive Information to any third party, except for those of its employees, lawyers, accountants, insurance agents and other professional representatives who have a need to know such Confidential Information for the purpose of COMPANY’S evaluation of the opportunity and who agree to abide by the terms of this Agreement. Except as necessary to evaluate the business opportunity, COMPANY shall not use, reproduce or draw upon the Confidentia1 information for any purpose or circulate it within its organization.
perturbation.justification: The changed text substitutes 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Information' at the beginning of the sentence, while retaining 'Confidential Information' later in the same sentence. This creates an ambiguity regarding whether 'Sensitive Information' is a subset of 'Confidential Information' or a separate category, leading to confusion about which information is protected. The contradicted text includes both terms, implying a potential distinction between them, thus confirming the contradiction introduced by the modified text as defined in the Perturbation Explanation. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition, specifically the contract using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The confidentiality obligations of COMPANY with respect to the Confidential Information shall terminate with respect to any particular portion of the Confidential Information that: (i) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available to the public, by publication, commercial use or otherwise, through no fault of COMPANY; (ii) was known by COMPANY at the time of disclosure; (iii) was obtained by COMPANY from a third party, free of any obligation of confidence; or (iv) was independently developed by COMPANY without the use of any of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The confidentiality obligations of COMPANY with respect to the Confidential Data shall terminate with respect to any particular portion of the Confidential Information that: (i) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available to the public, by publication, commercial use or otherwise, through no fault of COMPANY; (ii) was known by COMPANY at the time of disclosure; (iii) was obtained by COMPANY from a third party, free of any obligation of confidence; or (iv) was independently developed by COMPANY without the use of any of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' at the beginning of the sentence, while the rest of the paragraph and document refers to 'Confidential Information' creates an internal contradiction. It's ambiguous if 'Confidential Data' and 'Confidential Information' are synonymous, or if 'Confidential Data' has different criteria for termination of obligations, affecting enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The confidentiality obligations of COMPANY with respect to the Confidential Data shall terminate with respect to any particular portion of the Confidential Information that: (i) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available to the public, by publication, commercial use or otherwise, through no fault of COMPANY; (ii) was known by COMPANY at the time of disclosure; (iii) was obtained by COMPANY from a third party, free of any obligation of confidence; or (iv) was independently developed by COMPANY without the use of any of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Data' at the beginning of the sentence in the Changed Text. The rest of the paragraph and the Contradicted Text both refer to 'Confidential Information'. This creates a contradiction because it introduces ambiguity regarding whether 'Confidential Data' and 'Confidential Information' are synonymous. If they are not, the criteria for termination of confidentiality obligations would differ between the Changed Text and the Contradicted Text, affecting enforcement, as described in the explanation. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: COMPANY shall provide notice to DISCLOSING PARTY of any demand made upon it under lawful process to disclose or provide the Confidential Information. COMPANY agrees to cooperate with DISCLOSING PARTY if DISCLOSING PARTY elects to seek reasonable protective arrangements or oppose such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: COMPANY shall provide notice to DISCLOSING PARTY of any demand made upon it under lawful process to disclose or provide the Protected Information. COMPANY agrees to cooperate with DISCLOSING PARTY if DISCLOSING PARTY elects to seek reasonable protective arrangements or oppose such disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Information' creates an ambiguity concerning whether 'Protected Information' is a subset, superset, or entirely distinct category from 'Confidential Information.' This change impacts the obligations of COMPANY in case of a disclosure demand, leaving it uncertain what information requires notification and cooperation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information shall remain the property of DISCLOSING PARTY. This Agreement does not grant any rights to COMPANY with respect to the Confidential Information. Upon written notice from DISCLOSING PARTY at any time, COMPANY shall return to DISCLOSING PARTY or destroy all Confidential Information and any copies or derivative works thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information', which the contradicted text also refers to, defining it as the property of the DISCLOSING PARTY. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Information' in one instance. This creates a contradiction because the contract now uses two terms, 'Confidential Information' and 'Protected Information,' without defining the relationship between them, thus leading to uncertainty as to whether the obligations in the changed text apply to all 'Confidential Information' as described in the contradicted text, or only to some other set of information. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information shall remain the property of DISCLOSING PARTY. This Agreement does not grant any rights to COMPANY with respect to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: All Private Information shall remain the property of DISCLOSING PARTY. This Agreement does not grant any rights to COMPANY with respect to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Substituting 'Confidential Information' with 'Private Information' in the first sentence, while retaining 'Confidential Information' in the second sentence, creates confusion as to which type of information remains the property of the Disclosing Party and what rights the Company has. This ambiguity impacts the ownership and usage rights related to the disclosed data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Private Information shall remain the property of DISCLOSING PARTY. This Agreement does not grant any rights to COMPANY with respect to the Confidential Information. Upon written notice from DISCLOSING PARTY at any time, COMPANY shall return to DISCLOSING PARTY or destroy all Confidential Information and any copies or derivative works thereof.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Private Information' in one instance but retains 'Confidential Information' in another within the same sentence and in the contradicted text. This creates an ambiguity as to which type of information is being referred to, directly conflicting with the expectation of clarity and consistency regarding the handling of information and aligning with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties with respect to Confidential Information and no modification or waiver of the terms of this Agreement shall be binding unless it is in writing signed by an authorized representative of each party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties with respect to Classified Information and no modification or waiver of the terms of this Agreement shall be binding unless it is in writing signed by an authorized representative of each party.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Classified Information' introduces a new term without definition. It raises the question of whether obligations for 'Classified Information' are the same, lesser, or greater than 'Confidential Information,' potentially creating loopholes or ambiguities in enforcing the non-disclosure agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In connection with the business opportunity currently being explored by DISCLOSING PARTY and COMPANY, COMPANY agrees that all information obtained by it, whether in tangible or intangible form, from or about DISCLOSING PARTY, including, without limitation, information relating to the business opportunity being explored, or information relating to DISCLOSING PARTY’S products, services, technology, marketing, business plans, finances, research, development, know-how or personnel shall be considered Confidential Information. For purposes of this Agreement, Confidential Information shall also include the fact that such discussions are taking place.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information,' which is clearly defined in the 'Contradicted Text' section, specifying what constitutes Confidential Information. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Classified Information,' which is undefined within the contract. This creates a contradiction because the contract now uses a term ('Classified Information') without specifying its meaning or obligations, while simultaneously defining 'Confidential Information.' This introduces ambiguity and potential conflict, making it unclear whether the obligations in the 'Contradicted Text' section apply to 'Classified Information,' fulfilling the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition by using terms interchangeably without defining them.
